title: Buxa Fort
tagline: Buxa Fort — heritage city of West Bengal

overview.short: Buxa Fort is located at an altitude of 867 metres (2,844 ft) in the Buxa Tiger Reserve, in the Kalchini CD block in the Alipurduar subdivision of the Alipurduar district in West Be
overview.description: Buxa Fort is located at an altitude of 867 metres (2,844 ft) in the Buxa Tiger Reserve, in the Kalchini CD block in the Alipurduar subdivision of the Alipurduar district in West Bengal, India. It is 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Alipurduar, the nearest town. The King of Bhutan used the fort to protect territory connecting Tibet with India, via Bhutan. During the unrest in the annexation of Tibet by the People's Republic of China, hundreds of displaced persons used the abandoned fort as a place of refuge.

topPlaces.name: Jayanti, Alipurduar
topPlaces.description: Jayanti is a village in the Kalchini CD block in the Alipurduar subdivision of the Alipurduar district in West Bengal, India. It is a tourist destination of Dooars.

topPlaces.name: Dala Gewog
topPlaces.description: Dala Gewog (Dzongkha: དར་ལ་,Darla Gewog) is a gewog (village group) of Chukha District, Bhutan. The gewog has an area of around 140 km² and contains 7 villages.

topPlaces.name: Raimatang
topPlaces.description: Raimatang is a village in the Kalchini CD block in the Alipurduar subdivision of the Alipurduar district in West Bengal, India. It is about 45 kilometres (28 mi) away from Alipurduar and is a tourist destination.

items.note: Jayanti is a village in the Kalchini CD block in the Alipurduar subdivision of the Alipurduar district in West Bengal, India. It is a tourist destination of Dooars.
Dala Gewog (Dzongkha: དར་ལ་,Darla Gewog) is a gewog (village group) of Chukha District, Bhutan. The gewog has an area of around 140 km² and contains 7 villages.

Raimatang is a village in the Kalchini CD block in the Alipurduar subdivision of the Alipurduar district in West Bengal, India. It is about 45 kilometres (28 mi) away from Alipurduar and is a tourist destination.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Buxa Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Buxa Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Buxa Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pakyong Airport, Gangtok (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Alipurduar Junction (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Buxa Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Buxa Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Buxa Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Buxa Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Buxa Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jayanti, Alipurduar, Dala Gewog, Raimatang.
